description: Phase 0 — Bootstrap monorepo with Docker Compose: Vue 3 + TypeScript frontend (Vite HMR), Node.js + TypeScript backend (Express + tsx --watch), and MongoDB container with empty collections. Single command docker compose up starts the full stack.

userStories.title: Monorepo folder structure and base files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean monorepo layout so that frontend, backend, and infrastructure are clearly separated.
acceptanceCriteria: Root contains: docker-compose.yml (empty placeholder), ui/, backend/, .gitignore, .env.example
ui/ contains a Vite + Vue 3 + TypeScript scaffold created with yarn (package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, src/main.ts, src/App.vue)
backend/ contains a Node.js + TypeScript + Express skeleton (package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts)
Root .gitignore covers node_modules, dist, .env, .DS_Store
.env.example documents: MONGO_URI=mongodb://mongo:27017/surfsapp, PORT=3000, NODE_ENV=development
Typecheck passes in ui/ (yarn typecheck)
Typecheck passes in backend/ (npm run typecheck)
userStories.notes: This is the foundation — all other stories depend on this structure existing.

userStories.title: Frontend Dockerfile and Vite HMR configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Vue 3 frontend to reflect code changes instantly inside Docker so I don't need to rebuild the container.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/Dockerfile exists using a Node.js base image (single-stage)
vite.config.ts sets server.host: '0.0.0.0' and server.watch: { usePolling: true } for Docker bind-mount compatibility
App.vue renders a minimal placeholder page with the text 'surfsapp'
TypeScript strict mode enabled in ui/tsconfig.json
yarn typecheck passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Bind-mount strategy: source code mounted from host, node_modules in a named Docker volume.

userStories.title: Backend Dockerfile, Express skeleton, and health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Express backend to reload on file changes inside Docker so I can iterate without rebuilding.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/Dockerfile exists using a Node.js base image (single-stage)
backend/src/index.ts starts an Express server on the port from process.env.PORT (default 3000)
GET /health returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: '<ISO string>' } with HTTP 200
tsx --watch src/index.ts is the dev start command (npm run dev)
CORS configured to allow requests from http://localhost:5173
TypeScript strict mode enabled in backend/tsconfig.json
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Mongoose installed as a dependency ready for Phase 3 models. Graceful SIGTERM shutdown included.

userStories.title: MongoDB connection and init script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MongoDB to start with the required empty collections already created so Phase 3 can begin seeding immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/src/db/connection.ts exports a connectDB() function that connects via MONGO_URI env var using mongoose
connectDB() is called on server startup in index.ts and logs 'MongoDB connected' on success
backend/src/db/mongo-init.js creates three empty collections on first run: beaches, forecastsnapshots, settings
Init script is idempotent — running twice does not throw errors
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: mongo-init.js is plain JS (not TS) — it runs inside the MongoDB container via /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/.

userStories.title: Docker Compose orchestration with networking and volumes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want docker compose up to start all three services so I don't need to manage them individually.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml defines three services: ui, backend, mongo
ui service: builds from ui/Dockerfile, bind-mounts ui/ source, node_modules in named volume, port 5173:5173
backend service: builds from backend/Dockerfile, bind-mounts backend/ source, node_modules in named volume, port 3000:3000, depends_on: mongo
mongo service: uses mongo:7 image, port 27017:27017, named volume mongo-data for persistence, mounts backend/src/db/mongo-init.js to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/
All three services share a bridge network named surfsapp-network
Environment variables loaded from .env file
docker compose up starts all three services without errors
docker compose down stops and removes containers cleanly
userStories.notes: Bind-mount for source + named volume for node_modules is the hot-reload strategy for both ui and backend.

userStories.title: Inter-service connectivity verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to confirm all services can reach each other so I know the network is wired correctly before building features.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend GET /health response includes { status: 'ok', mongo: 'connected', timestamp: '<ISO string>' } when MongoDB is running
Backend GET /health response includes { status: 'ok', mongo: 'disconnected' } when MongoDB is not available (graceful degradation)
curl http://localhost:3000/health returns HTTP 200 with the expected JSON
curl http://localhost:5173 returns HTTP 200 (Vite dev server responding)
No CORS errors when frontend fetch('http://localhost:3000/health') is called from the browser
Typecheck passes in backend/ (npm run typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the final verification story. All previous stories must pass before this one is attempted.
